extensionDescription.message: One-click Markdown web clipper. Save articles, docs, code & tables as clean Markdown for AI agents & LLMs. Supports Obsidian & more.

actionTitle.description: Default title for the extension action

cmdCopyTab.message: Copy current tab as Markdown to the clipboard

cmdCopySelection.message: Copy current selection as Markdown to the clipboard

cmdCopyTabLink.message: Copy current tab URL as Markdown link to the clipboard

cmdCopySelectedTabLink.message: Copy selected tabs URL as Markdown link to the clipboard

cmdSendToObsidianSelection.message: Copy current selection as Markdown to Obsidian

cmdSendToObsidianTab.message: Copy current tab as Markdown to Obsidian

contextCopyTabLink.message: Copy Tab URL as Markdown Link

contextCopyAllTabLinks.message: Copy All Tab URLs as Markdown Link List

contextCopySelectedTabLinks.message: Copy Selected Tab URLs as Markdown Link List

contextPickElement.description: Context menu item for activating manual element picker mode

notifSupportMessage.message: MarkSnip has helped export over $MILESTONE$ pages. If it has been useful, support ongoing development.

notifReviewMessage.message: A quick review helps others discover MarkSnip and takes less than a minute.

popupContentLabel.description: Label for content scope segmented control

popupPickElementBtn.description: Button label for activating manual element picker mode

popupPickElementStarting.description: Temporary popup feedback after activating element picker mode

popupPickElementFailed.description: Temporary popup feedback when element picker activation fails

popupAlertFailedToActivateElementPicker.message: Failed to activate element picker. Please try again.
popupAlertFailedToActivateElementPicker.description: Alert shown when element picker mode cannot be activated

popupDownloadTextBtn.description: Primary action label for plain text export

popupDownloadTextSelectionBtn.description: Selection action label for plain text export

popupDownloadHtmlBtn.description: Primary action label for HTML export

popupDownloadHtmlSelectionBtn.description: Selection action label for HTML export

popupDownloadPdfSelectionBtn.description: Selection action label for PDF export

popupExportPdfNote.description: Note next to Save as PDF option

popupUrlsPlaceholder.message:
https://example.com/page1
https://example.com/page2

Or use markdown links:
[Page Title](https://example.com)

popupBatchSaveZip.description: Toggle option for batch save mode

popupBatchSaveIndividual.description: Toggle option for batch save mode

popupAlertNoLinksSelected.message: No links selected. Please select elements containing links before clicking Done.
popupAlertNoLinksSelected.description: Alert message when no links are selected in picker

popupAlertFailedToActivateLinkPicker.message: Failed to activate link picker. Please try again.
popupAlertFailedToActivateLinkPicker.description: Alert message when link picker fails to activate

optionsSectionTemplatesDesc.message: Configure title, front-matter, and back-matter templates for your clipped markdown files.

optionsTemplateVarRefTitle.description: Summary title for variable reference details

optionsDisallowedCharsDesc.message: Characters to strip or replace in title/filename - in addition to /, ?, <, >, \\, :, *, |, "

optionsFrontmatterInput.message: Text that appears at the top of the output file

optionsPreviewHint.message: Preview uses example metadata and the current date/time.

optionsBackmatterInput.message: Text that appears at the bottom of the output file

optionsIncludeTemplateToggle.message: Append front/back template to clipped text

optionsIncludeTemplateHint.message: Same as the popup's Template toggle. When enabled, each clip includes your Front-matter and Back-matter templates.

optionsSectionAppearanceDesc.message: Customize the look of the MarkSnip popup.

optionsShowThemeToggle.message: Show quick theme toggle in popup header

optionsShowThemeToggleHint.message: Adds a header button in the popup for one-click switching between light and dark mode.

optionsSpecialThemesDesc.message: Apply a tailored aesthetic across the popup, options page, guide, and editor.

optionsColorBlindHint.message: Uses a WCAG AA palette across the popup, options page, guide, and editor.

optionsShowThemeIconHint.message: Display the mascot icon in the header when using supported special themes.

optionsAccentThemeNote.message: The active special theme controls accent color while active.

optionsEditorThemeDesc.message: Choose a syntax highlighting theme for the Markdown editor in the popup.

optionsEditorThemeLockNote.message: The active special theme provides its own light and dark editor themes while active.

optionsEditorThemeDarkOnlyNote.message: Dark mode only — falls back to Default in light mode

optionsCompactModeHint.message: Hides quick settings panel and reduces padding for a minimal popup. The settings icon remains accessible in the header.

optionsShowUserGuideIconHint.message: Displays the User Guide button in the popup header for quick access to help.

optionsSectionExportsDesc.message: Configure how files are exported and manage integrations with Obsidian, assistants, and the Agent Bridge.

optionsEnableContextMenusHint.message: Shows right-click actions for clipping pages, selected text, links, and tabs.

optionsEnableBatchProcessingHint.message: Shows the Batch Processing entry in the popup and allows bulk URL conversion.

optionsDefaultPopupActionDesc.message: Controls the popup's main action button and selection action button only. Context menus, shortcuts, batch exports, library exports, Agent Bridge, and Obsidian remain Markdown-based.

optionsDownloadModeDesc.message: Method to use for downloading Markdown files. Set to "Content Link" if you're having trouble with the Downloads API (Sometimes conflicts can occur with other download extensions, leading to randomly generated filenames and other symptoms).

optionsDownloadModeNote.message: Note: "Content Link" mode disables some functionality such as downloading images or using subfolders in the filename.

optionsDownloadImagesHint.message: In Downloads API mode, image files are saved too and markdown image links are rewritten to those local files.

optionsClipsFolderLabel.message: Folder inside Downloads/ to store MarkSnip clips

optionsDefaultAssistantTargetDesc.message: Used when the popup's main action is set to Send to Assistant. If a selected custom target is removed, MarkSnip falls back to ChatGPT.

optionsAssistantTargetsDesc.message: ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity are built in. Add custom HTTPS URLs that accept a {prompt} placeholder in the query string.

optionsAssistantTargetHint.message: Use exactly one {prompt} placeholder. Example: https://example.com/new?q={prompt}

optionsAssistantUrlLengthDesc.message: Advanced setting for Send to Assistant links. If a generated assistant URL exceeds this value, MarkSnip copies the Markdown to your clipboard and opens the assistant landing page instead. Default: 3600. Changing this is not recommended.

optionsObsidianIntegrationDesc.message: For integration with Obsidian, you need to install and enable the community plugin Advanced Obsidian URI. This plugin helps bypass character limitations in URLs by using the clipboard as the source for creating new files. Images sent this way stay as remote links; attachment files are not copied into your vault.

guideQuickStart1.message: Click the MarkSnip icon in the browser toolbar to open the popup.

guideQuickStart2.message: The current page is run through Mozilla Readability to extract the main content, then converted to Markdown with Turndown.

guideQuickStart3.message: Edit, copy, or download the Markdown using the built-in CodeMirror editor.

guideQuickStartTip.message: Tip: Use Alt+Shift+M to open the popup without clicking the icon.

guideWelcomeDesc1.message: Thanks for installing MarkSnip. This guide will help you get started with clipping web pages as clean Markdown.

guideWelcomeDesc2.message: Start with the Quick Start section below, or browse the table of contents on the left.

guideFaq1Title.message: The popup shows "Processing page..." and nothing happens

guideFaq1Desc.message: Some pages block content scripts. Try refreshing the page, or check that MarkSnip has permission to access the site. Internal browser pages (chrome://, about:) cannot be clipped.

guideFaq2Desc.message: Make sure Download Mode is set to "Downloads API" and the Images toggle is enabled. Content Link mode does not support image downloads.

guideFaq3Title.message: Send to Obsidian is not visible

guideFaq3Desc.message: Enable Obsidian Integration in Settings. You also need the Advanced Obsidian URI community plugin installed in Obsidian.

optionsObsidianVaultPlaceholder.message: If blank, it will use the main Vault. Please specify it.

optionsAgentBridgeDesc.message: Install the local MarkSnip companion once, then let local tools pull the current page's Markdown directly without using downloads.

optionsAgentBridgeEnableHint.message: When off, MarkSnip will not open a local companion connection.

optionsAgentBridgePermPreflightTitle.description: Title for Agent Bridge permission preflight

optionsAgentBridgePermPreflightDesc.message: MarkSnip is about to ask the browser for permission to talk to the local MarkSnip companion.
optionsAgentBridgePermPreflightDesc.description: Description for Agent Bridge permission preflight

optionsAgentBridgePermBullet3.message: Regular clipping still works without this

optionsAgentBridgePermContinue.description: Continue button for Agent Bridge permission

optionsAgentBridgePermCancel.description: Cancel button for Agent Bridge permission

optionsAgentBridgePermGuide.description: Link to setup guide for Agent Bridge permission

optionsAgentBridgePermDeniedDesc.message: Permission wasn't granted. Agent Bridge stays off. Regular clipping is unchanged.
optionsAgentBridgePermDeniedDesc.description: Description when Agent Bridge permission is denied

optionsAgentBridgePermRetry.description: Retry button for Agent Bridge permission

optionsAgentBridgeStatusTextDisabled.description: Disabled status text for Agent Bridge

optionsAgentBridgeStatusHint.message: Enable the Agent Bridge to let MarkSnip connect to the local companion.

optionsAgentBridgeCheckStatus.description: Button to check Agent Bridge connection status

optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideTitle.description: Title for Agent Bridge setup guide

optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep1.message: To connect MarkSnip to local CLI tools, follow these steps:
optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep1.description: Step 1 of Agent Bridge setup guide

optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep2a.message: Download the companion archive for your OS from the
optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep2a.description: Step 2a of Agent Bridge setup guide

optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep2b.description: Step 2b of Agent Bridge setup guide

optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep3.message: Run the install command to register the native messaging host:
optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep3.description: Step 3 of Agent Bridge setup guide

optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep4.message: Enable the toggle above and MarkSnip will connect automatically.
optionsAgentBridgeSetupGuideStep4.description: Step 4 of Agent Bridge setup guide

optionsAgentBridgeSetupNote.message: Local-only feature. Bridge settings are not exported and do not sync between machines.

optionsLibrarySectionDesc.message: Manage the popup Library that stores saved clips locally on this browser only.

optionsLibraryEnableHint.message: Shows the Library view in the popup. Turning it off hides the feature without deleting saved items.

optionsLibraryAutoSaveLabel.message: Save pages automatically when popup opens

optionsLibraryAutoSaveHint.message: When off, the popup Library shows a manual Save Clip button instead.

optionsLibraryItemsToKeepTitle.description: Title for Library Items to Keep

optionsLibraryItemsToKeepDesc.message: Maximum number of saved Library clips to keep locally. Lowering this trims older items immediately.
optionsLibraryItemsToKeepDesc.description: Description for Library Items to Keep

optionsLibraryItemsToKeepNote.message: Library settings are included in settings import/export. Saved Library items remain local-only and are excluded.
optionsLibraryItemsToKeepNote.description: Note for Library Items to Keep

optionsLibraryClearDesc.message: Delete all saved Library items from local storage. This cannot be undone.

optionsMarkdownSectionDesc.message: Configure how HTML is converted to Markdown syntax.

optionsPreserveCodeFormattingLabel.description: Label to preserve code block HTML formatting

optionsPreserveCodeFormattingHint.message: When enabled, preserves original HTML formatting within code blocks. This can be useful for maintaining exact visual formatting, but may make the Markdown harder to read and use in other tools. When disabled (default), produces clean code blocks.
optionsPreserveCodeFormattingHint.description: Hint to preserve code block HTML formatting

optionsAutoDetectCodeLanguageLabel.description: Label to auto-detect code block language

optionsAutoDetectCodeLanguageHint.message: Uses highlight.js to infer fenced code block language when no explicit language is present. Disable this if snippets are frequently labeled with incorrect languages.
optionsAutoDetectCodeLanguageHint.description: Hint to auto-detect code block language

optionsImageStyleOriginalSource.description: Option for Original Source image style

optionsImageStyleNote1.message: Note: The following only apply if Download Images is on.
optionsImageStyleNote1.description: Note for image styles when downloading images

optionsImageStyleMarkdown.description: Option for Pure Markdown image style

optionsImageStyleBase64.description: Option for Base64 Encoded image style

optionsImageStyleObsidian.description: Option for Obsidian Internal Embed image style

optionsImageStyleObsidianNoFolder.description: Option for Obsidian Embed without folder

optionsImageRefStyleNote.message: Note: The following only apply if Image Style is markdown (not Obsidian-styled).

optionsTurndownEscapeHint.message: By default, backslashes (\) are used to escape Markdown characters in the HTML input. This ensures that these characters are not interpreted as Markdown. For example, the contents of <h1>1. Hello world</h1> needs to be escaped to 1\. Hello world, otherwise it will be interpreted as a list item rather than a heading. Disabling this option disables this escaping.

optionsSkipHiddenContentHint.message: Ignore content hidden with CSS, the hidden attribute, or aria-hidden="true". Disable this to include collapsed accordions and other hidden DOM content in clips.

optionsHashtagHandlingDesc.message: Control how hashtag-like words (for example #AI) are written in clipped text. Useful for tools like Obsidian that treat hashtags as tags.

optionsTablesSectionDesc.message: Configure how HTML tables are converted to Markdown tables.

optionsTableStripLinksLabel.description: Label to strip links from tables

optionsTableStripLinksHint.message: Removes all links from tables, keeping only the text content. Creates cleaner, more readable tables.
optionsTableStripLinksHint.description: Hint to strip links from tables

optionsTableStripFormattingLabel.description: Label to strip formatting from tables

optionsTableStripFormattingHint.message: Removes formatting like bold and italic from table cells. Results in plain text tables.
optionsTableStripFormattingHint.description: Hint to strip formatting from tables

optionsTablePrettyPrintHint.message: Formats tables with proper spacing and alignment. Makes tables more readable in markdown.

optionsTableCenterTextLabel.description: Label to center text in table columns

optionsTableCenterTextHint.message: Centers text within table columns. Creates a more polished look.
optionsTableCenterTextHint.description: Hint to center text in table columns

optionsSiteRulesSectionDesc.message: Create ordered wildcard URL rules that override clip settings for matching sites. The first enabled match wins.

optionsSiteRulesListDesc.message: Use patterns like example.com/*, docs.example.com/blog/*, or *.example.com/*. Query strings and hashes are ignored.

optionsSiteRulesEmptyTitle.description: Title when no site rules exist

optionsSiteRulesEmptyDesc.message: Add a rule when you want different templates, image behavior, or table formatting for specific domains.
optionsSiteRulesEmptyDesc.description: Description when no site rules exist

optionsSiteRuleEditorHint.message: Missing scheme matches both http and https. Missing path implies /*.

optionsSiteRuleEnabledLabel.description: Label for Site Rule Enabled state

optionsSiteRuleNoOverrides.description: Empty state label for a Site Rule without overrides

optionsSiteRuleInvalidPattern.description: Prefix for invalid Site Rule pattern errors

optionsSiteRuleMoveUpBtn.description: Button to move a Site Rule up

optionsSiteRuleMoveDownBtn.description: Button to move a Site Rule down

optionsSiteRuleEditBtn.description: Button to edit a Site Rule

optionsSiteRuleDeleteBtn.description: Button to delete a Site Rule

optionsSiteRuleEnabledHint.message: Disabled rules stay in the list but never match.
optionsSiteRuleEnabledHint.description: Hint for Site Rule Enabled state

optionsSiteRuleDownloadImagesLabel.description: Label for Site Rule Download Images

optionsSiteRuleKeepOriginalSource.description: Option to keep original image source

optionsSiteRuleCancelBtn.description: Button to cancel site rule editing

optionsImportExportSectionDesc.message: Back up your settings or restore from a previous backup.

optionsPermissionsPrivacyDesc.message: MarkSnip processes pages locally and only accesses page content when you trigger a clip.

optionsPermissionSiteAccessBullet1.message: Needed so MarkSnip can clip any page you ask it to clip.

optionsPermissionSiteAccessBullet2.message: Not used for passive browsing monitoring.

optionsPermissionSiteAccessBullet3.message: Page content is not sent to external servers.

optionsPermissionSiteAccessBullet4.message: This is managed by the browser as part of the extension install.

optionsPermissionLocalCompanionBullet2.message: Only talks to the local MarkSnip companion installed on this machine.

optionsPermissionLocalCompanionBullet4.message: Can be stopped by turning Agent Bridge off.

optionsImportDesc.message: Restore options from a previously exported backup file.

optionsImportBtn.description: Button to choose file for import

optionsExportDesc.message: Download your current options as a JSON file.

optionsResetDesc.message: Restore every setting to its factory default. This cannot be undone.

optionsSearchNoResultsPrefix.description: Prefix for no search results message

optionsSearchNoResultsSuffix.description: Suffix for no search results message

optionsSearchGuideInstead.description: Link text to search the guide instead

guideSearchHint.message: Try different keywords or browse the table of contents.
guideSearchHint.description: Hint when no search results found

guideTocUsingPopup.description: TOC link for Using the Popup

guideTocContextMenus.description: TOC link for Context Menus & Shortcuts

guideUsingPopupHeading.description: Heading for Using the Popup section

guideUsingPopupQuickSettingsDesc1.message: At the top of the popup you'll find two toggle switches:

guideUsingPopupQuickSettingsImg.message: Images — when on, images are downloaded alongside your Markdown file (requires Downloads API mode).

guideUsingPopupQuickSettingsTpl.message: Template — when on, the front-matter and back-matter templates are prepended/appended to the output.

guideUsingPopupScopeDesc.message: Below the toggles is a segmented control with two buttons:

guideUsingPopupScopeSel.message: Selection — clips only the text you selected on the page before opening the popup.

guideUsingPopupScopeDoc.message: Document — clips the entire page content (default if nothing is selected).

guideUsingPopupTitle.description: Heading for Editing the Title subsection

guideUsingPopupTitleDesc.message: The Title field determines the filename of the downloaded Markdown file. It is populated automatically from the title template (configurable in options) but you can edit it freely before saving.

guideUsingPopupPreviewDesc1.message: The main area of the popup is a syntax-highlighted Markdown editor (powered by CodeMirror). You can make quick edits here before downloading.

guideUsingPopupPreviewDesc2.message: Above the editor are two buttons:

guideUsingPopupPreviewCopyAll.message: Copy All — copies all the Markdown to your clipboard.

guideUsingPopupPreviewCopySel.message: Copy Selection — appears when you select text inside the editor, allowing you to copy just the highlighted portion.

guideUsingPopupActionsMain.message: Uses your configured popup default action. Choose Markdown (.md), Plain text (.txt), HTML (.html), PDF, or Send to Assistant.

guideUsingPopupActionsSel.message: Appears when text is selected inside the editor; exports or sends only the selected text using the same popup default action.

guideUsingPopupActionsObsidian.message: Copies Markdown to clipboard and opens Obsidian via the Advanced URI plugin to create a new note (only visible when Obsidian integration is enabled).

guideUsingPopupActionsNote.message: Note: This popup default action setting only affects popup actions. Context menus, keyboard download shortcuts, batch exports, Library exports, Agent Bridge, and Obsidian actions remain Markdown-based.

guideBatchProcessingDesc.message: Click the batch icon in the popup header to switch to batch mode.

guideBatchAddUrlsDesc.message: Paste URLs into the text area, one per line. You can also use Markdown link syntax:

guideBatchPickLinks.description: Heading for Pick Links from Page subsection

guideBatchPickLinksDesc.message: Click Pick Links from Page to enter the visual link picker. This injects an overlay onto the current page that lets you click on links to select them. A floating toolbar shows how many links you've selected, and a Done button sends them back to the batch URL list.
guideBatchPickLinksDesc.description: Description for Pick Links from Page

guideBatchOutputZip.message: ZIP file — all converted Markdown files are bundled into a single .zip download.

guideBatchOutputIndiv.message: Individual — each page is downloaded as a separate .md file.

guideBatchOutputDesc.message: Click Convert All URLs to start processing. A progress bar shows the current status including a count and the URL being processed.

guideContextMenusHeading.description: Heading for Context Menus & Shortcuts section

guideContextMenusDesc.message: Right-click on a page to access MarkSnip actions under the context menu. These are available when Enable Context Menus is turned on in settings.

guideContextDownloadTab.message: Downloads the current tab as a Markdown file without opening the popup.

guideContextDownloadSel.message: Downloads the highlighted text as Markdown.

guideContextDownloadAll.message: Downloads every open tab in the current window as Markdown files.

guideContextCopyTab.message: Converts the entire page to Markdown and copies it to the clipboard.

guideContextCopySel.message: Converts the selection to Markdown and copies it.

guideContextCopyAllUrls.message: Copies all open tab URLs as a Markdown link list.

guideContextCopySelUrls.message: Copies URLs from multi-selected tabs (<kbd>Ctrl</kbd>/<kbd>Cmd</kbd>) as a link list.

guideContextObsidianDesc.message: These items appear only when Obsidian integration is enabled:

guideContextObsidianSel.message: Sends the selection to Obsidian as a new note.
guideContextObsidianSel.description: Send Text Selection to Obsidian description

guideContextObsidianTab.message: Sends the full page to Obsidian as a new note.

guideContextObsidianNote.message: At the bottom of the context menu you'll also find checkboxes for Include front/back template and Download Images.

guideContextShortcutsLink.message: Copy current tab URL as Markdown link

guideContextShortcutsExtra.message: Additional commands without default shortcuts (assign your own in the browser's shortcut settings):

guideContextShortcutsExtra2.message: Copy selected tabs as Markdown link list

guideSettingsDesc.message: Open the options page by clicking the gear icon in the popup header, or by right-clicking the MarkSnip icon and selecting Options.

guideSettingsTemplatesTitleDesc.message: Controls the popup's title field and the resulting filename. Uses Custom Text Substitutions. Default: {pageTitle}

guideSettingsTemplatesFolderDesc.message: A subfolder within your browser's downloads folder where Markdown files are saved. Only available in Downloads API mode. Uses text substitutions.

guideSettingsTemplatesCharsDesc.message: Characters automatically stripped from filenames, in addition to system-reserved characters. Default: []#^ (for Obsidian compatibility).

guideSettingsTemplatesFrontBackDesc.message: Text prepended or appended to every clipped Markdown file. Useful for YAML front-matter or other metadata. Uses text substitutions.

guideSettingsDownloadsMenusDesc.message: Toggles the right-click context menu items on and off. Use Visible Menu Items to choose the individual MarkSnip actions that are allowed to appear. Browsers only show items that apply to where you right-click, such as page, selection, link, image, or tab menus. Default: On, with all menu items visible.

guideSettingsDownloadsActionDesc.message: Controls the popup's primary action buttons. Choose Markdown, Plain text, HTML, PDF, or Send to Assistant. This does not change context-menu downloads, keyboard download shortcuts, batch output, Library exports, Agent Bridge output, or Obsidian sends.

guideSettingsDownloadsTargetDesc.message: When Send to Assistant is selected, choose the popup's default destination such as ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, or one of your custom assistant URLs.

guideSettingsDownloadsTargetsDesc.message: ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity are built in. Add custom HTTPS assistant URLs that accept a {prompt} placeholder in the query string. MarkSnip inserts the current Markdown into that placeholder when you send from the popup.

guideSettingsDownloadsLength.description: Subheading for Assistant URL Length Cap

guideSettingsDownloadsLengthDesc.message: Controls how long a generated assistant URL can be before MarkSnip falls back to copying the Markdown to your clipboard and opening the assistant landing page instead. The default is 3600, and changing it is not recommended.
guideSettingsDownloadsLengthDesc.description: Description for Assistant URL Length Cap

guideSettingsMarkdownPreserve.description: Subheading for Preserve Code Block Formatting

guideSettingsMarkdownPreserveDesc.message: When enabled, preserves the original HTML formatting inside code blocks. Default: Off.
guideSettingsMarkdownPreserveDesc.description: Description for Preserve Code Block Formatting

guideSettingsMarkdownLinkDesc.message: Inlined (default): [Google](http://google.com). Referenced: separate link definitions at the bottom. Strip Links: removes links entirely.

guideSettingsMarkdownEscapeDesc.message: Backslash-escapes special Markdown characters to prevent misinterpretation. Default: On.

guideSettingsMarkdownHashtagDesc.message: Keep (default), Remove #, or Escape #. Only affects article text; Markdown syntax and code are preserved.

guideSettingsTablesStripLinksDesc.message: Removes hyperlinks from table cells, keeping only the text.

guideSettingsTablesStripFormDesc.message: Removes bold, italic, and other formatting from table cells.

guideSettingsTablesPrettyDesc.message: Adds proper spacing and column alignment.

guideSettingsImportExportImport.message: Import — restore settings from a previously exported JSON file.

guideSettingsImportExportExport.message: Export — save all current settings to a JSON file for backup or transfer to another browser.

guideObsidianIntegrationDesc.message: Enable this to show the Send to Obsidian button and Obsidian context menu actions. Requires the Advanced Obsidian URI community plugin.

guideObsidianIntegrationVault.message: Vault Name — leave blank for the default vault.

guideObsidianIntegrationNote.message: Note: Images sent via Obsidian stay as remote links. The extension does not place attachment files inside the vault.

guideObsidianModeApiDesc.message: Uses the browser's download API. Supports subfolders, image downloads, and Save As dialogs.

guideObsidianModeLinkDesc.message: Falls back to a content link for downloading. More limited — disables image downloads and subfolders.

guideObsidianModeSaveAs.description: Subheading for Show Save As Dialog

guideObsidianModeSaveAsDesc.message: Forces the browser's Save As dialog for every download. Not recommended when Download Images is on.
guideObsidianModeSaveAsDesc.description: Description for Show Save As Dialog

guideObsidianImageDesc.message: When enabled (Downloads API mode only), images are downloaded alongside the Markdown file with local references.

guideObsidianImagePrefixDesc.message: A prefix or subfolder for downloaded images. Uses text substitutions. Including a / creates a subfolder. Default: {pageTitle}/.

guidePermissionsIntro.message: MarkSnip processes pages entirely within your browser. It does not send page content to any external server. This section explains what browser permissions MarkSnip uses, why they are needed, and when they are actually exercised.

guidePermissionsSiteDesc.message: MarkSnip requests access to web pages so it can clip pages across the web when you trigger a clip. This covers popup clipping, keyboard shortcuts, context-menu actions, multi-tab link actions, and batch processing of URLs opened in new tabs.

guidePermissionsSiteBullet1.message: Only active when you clip — MarkSnip does not monitor browsing activity or read pages in the background.

guidePermissionsSiteBullet2.message: Processed locally — page content is converted to Markdown inside your browser and is never sent to a remote service.

guidePermissionsSiteBullet3.message: Managed by the browser — you can review this permission in your browser’s extension settings at any time.

guidePermissionsOtherDesc.message: Beyond site access, MarkSnip uses a small set of standard browser permissions to make its main features work. These permissions support local browser actions; they do not change the fact that clipping stays local.

guidePermissionsOtherCol2.description: Table column Why MarkSnip uses it

guidePermissionsOtherActiveTabDesc.message: Lets MarkSnip work with the page you explicitly invoked it on.

guidePermissionsOtherActiveTabWhen.message: Opening the popup or using a shortcut on the current tab.

guidePermissionsOtherDownloadsDesc.message: Saves Markdown files, ZIP exports, and downloaded images through the browser.

guidePermissionsOtherClipboardDesc.message: Writes generated Markdown to your clipboard.

guidePermissionsOtherStorageDesc.message: Remembers your settings, templates, themes, and feature toggles.

guidePermissionsOtherStorageWhen.message: Whenever you save or restore settings.

guidePermissionsOtherContextMenusDesc.message: Adds right-click actions for pages, selections, links, images, and tabs.

guidePermissionsOtherContextMenusWhen.message: When context menus are enabled in settings.

guidePermissionsOtherScriptingDesc.message: Injects MarkSnip’s capture helpers into the page at clip time.

guidePermissionsOtherOffscreenDesc.message: Uses a hidden document for Manifest V3 clipboard and download workflows in Chromium browsers.

guidePermissionsOtherOffscreenWhen.message: Behind the scenes during some copy and download actions.

guidePermissionsBridgeDesc.message: The Agent Bridge feature requires an additional browser permission called Native Messaging. This is an optional permission — MarkSnip works perfectly without it.

guidePermissionsBridgeBullet1.message: What it does — lets MarkSnip talk to a small local companion program installed on your machine.

guidePermissionsBridgeBullet2.message: Local only — communication stays on your computer. No data leaves your machine.

guidePermissionsBridgeBullet3.message: When it’s used — only while Agent Bridge is enabled. Turning the toggle off stops all native messaging activity.

guidePermissionsBridgeBullet4.message: How to grant — enable the Agent Bridge toggle in Settings. MarkSnip will show an explanation before the browser’s permission prompt appears.

guidePermissionsBridgeBullet5.message: How to revoke — once granted, this permission cannot be selectively removed. To fully revoke it, you must reinstall the extension. Turning off the Agent Bridge toggle stops all native messaging activity without needing to revoke the permission.

guidePermissionsBridgeNote.message: Note: If you deny the permission, regular clipping is completely unaffected. You can always try again later.

guideFaq4Desc.message: Check that Enable Context Menus is turned on in Downloads settings.

guideFaq5Desc.message: Another extension may have claimed the same shortcut. Open your browser's extension shortcut settings to reassign keys.

guideFaq6Desc.message: Check the Markdown Options to adjust heading style, link style, code block style, and escape settings to match your workflow.

guideTextSubIntro.message: The Title Template, Downloads Subfolder, Front/Back Templates, Image Filename Prefix, and Obsidian Folder Name all support these variables:

guideTextSubMetaPageTitle.message: Title of the actual page (<title> tag)

guideTextSubMetaLength.message: Length of the article in characters

guideTextSubUrlNote.message: All URL components are also available prefixed with page (e.g. {pageOrigin}, {pageHostname}).

guideTextSubDateDesc.message: Use {date:FORMAT} with Moment.js formatting tokens. Example: {date:YYYY-MM-DD}.

guideTextSubTagsDesc.message: Any <meta> tag value can be referenced, e.g. {og:image}, {og:description}.

guideTextSubParamsDesc.message: Change the casing of any text variable (except {date} and {keywords}) by appending a colon and style:

guideTextSubParamsNote.message: Note: Not all websites provide all metadata values. Missing values are replaced with empty strings.

guideOpenSettingsTitle.description: Tooltip for opening settings from the guide

guideSearchAriaLabel.description: ARIA label for guide search input

guideTocAriaLabel.description: ARIA label for guide table of contents

guideSearchResultsAriaLabel.description: ARIA label for guide search results region

guideSearchClearAriaLabel.description: ARIA label for guide search clear button

guideWelcomeDismissLabel.description: ARIA label for dismissing guide welcome message

guideSearchResultsCountZero.description: Guide search result count when there are no results

guideSearchResultsCountOne.description: Guide search result count when there is one result

guideSearchResultsCountMany.description: Guide search result count when there are multiple results

guideSummaryQuickStart.message: Get started with MarkSnip in three steps
guideSummaryQuickStart.description: Guide search summary for Quick Start

guideSummaryUsingPopup.message: The main popup interface for clipping pages
guideSummaryUsingPopup.description: Guide search summary for Using the Popup

guideSummaryQuickSettings.description: Guide search summary for Quick Settings

optionsSectionsAriaLabel.description: ARIA label for the options sidebar tab list

optionsTemplateVarRefIntro.message: For the title, as well as the front- and back-matter custom text, you can use the following text replacement values. Please note that not all websites will provide all values.
optionsTemplateVarRefIntro.description: Intro text for options template variable reference

optionsTemplateVarArticleTitle.description: Template variable reference for article title

optionsTemplateVarPageTitle.description: Template variable reference for page title

optionsTemplateVarLength.message: {length} - Length of the article, in characters
optionsTemplateVarLength.description: Template variable reference for article length

optionsTemplateVarExcerpt.message: {excerpt} - Article description or short excerpt from the content

optionsTemplateVarDir.description: Template variable reference for content direction

optionsTemplateVarDate.message: {date:FORMAT} - The current date and time. Check the format reference

optionsTemplateVarKeywords.message: {keywords} - Meta keywords (if present). Comma separated by default.

optionsTemplateVarKeywordsSeparator.message: {keywords:SEPARATOR} - Meta keywords separated by SEPARATOR. For example, to separate by space, use {keywords: }
optionsTemplateVarKeywordsSeparator.description: Template variable reference for custom keyword separator

optionsTemplateVarMetaTags.message: There is also support for all meta tags not mentioned above, should the page you are clipping support them. For example, try {og:image} or any other widely supported meta tags.
optionsTemplateVarMetaTags.description: Template variable reference for arbitrary meta tags

optionsTemplateVarUrlInfo.description: Heading for URL template variable reference

optionsTemplateVarBaseUri.description: Template variable reference for base URI

optionsTemplateVarPageUrl.message: {pageURL} / {tabURL} - Current address-bar URL of the tab.
optionsTemplateVarPageUrl.description: Template variable reference for page URL

optionsTemplateVarHost.message: {host} - The host from {baseURI} (hostname, and port if present).

optionsTemplateVarProtocol.message: {protocol} - The protocol scheme from {baseURI}, including the trailing :.

optionsTemplateVarPathname.message: {pathname} - The path from {baseURI}, not including query string or fragment.

optionsTemplateVarSearch.description: Template variable reference for query string

optionsTemplateVarPageComponents.message: {pageOrigin}, {pageHost}, {pageHostname}, {pagePort}, {pageProtocol}, {pagePathname}, {pageSearch}, {pageHash} - Same URL components as above, but from {pageURL}.
optionsTemplateVarPageComponents.description: Template variable reference for page URL components

optionsTemplateVarSpaExample.description: Heading for SPA route template variable example

optionsTemplateVarAddressBarUrl.description: Label for address bar URL example

optionsTemplateVarParsedBaseUri.description: Label for parsed base URI example

optionsTemplateVarStandardExample.message: Standard page example (no special base behavior):
optionsTemplateVarStandardExample.description: Heading for standard template variable example

optionsTemplateVarRecommendedSource.description: Template snippet for recommended source URL

optionsTemplateVarBaseSource.description: Template snippet for base source URL

optionsTemplateVarSourceParts.description: Template snippet for source URL parts

optionsTemplateVarParameterizationIntro.message: Additionally, you can parameterize any of the text variables (other than date and keywords) by using the following syntax:

optionsTemplateVarCamelDesc.message: camelCase: every word capital except the first word

optionsTemplateVarKebabDesc.message: kebab-case: hyphens between words, all lowercase

optionsTemplateVarSnakeDesc.message: snake_case: underscores between words, all lowercase

optionsTemplateVarAsciiDesc.message: ASCII only: strip accents and non-ASCII characters

optionsTemplateVarSlugifyDesc.message: URL slug: lowercase, ASCII, alphanumeric or hyphen
